name: glide-bedside
purpose: Repo-local orientation map for significant Glide Bedside changes. This file helps agents find and check important product, API, data, Alexa, and deployment surfaces, but it is not runtime authority or a replacement for source code, production state, docs, or Mission Control state.

authority.canonicalRuntime: Glide Bedside production app on Railway and its configured Postgres database

surfaces.label: Browser dashboard and family settings
surfaces.summary: Mobile-first caregiver dashboard, display mode, history, login, settings, theme, auth fetch, and client release/version behavior.
driftRisks: Dashboard workflows change without corresponding API or settings updates.
Version/accountability surfaces stop matching deployed release behavior.

surfaces.label: Express API and auth boundary
surfaces.summary: HTTP routes for health, version, dashboard data, settings, logging, backups, display access, machine API keys, sessions, and Clerk/Alexa auth boundaries.
driftRisks: New API routes bypass auth, family scoping, or release accountability.
Environment requirements change without README or .env.example updates.

surfaces.label: Postgres schema and patient/family data helpers
surfaces.summary: Database schema initialization, scoped query helpers, settings cache, sessions, entries, patients, families, and migration path from the retired SQLite shape.
driftRisks: Schema changes do not stay aligned with dashboard, bot, Alexa, or backup expectations.
Family/patient scoping regresses while adding multi-family behavior.

surfaces.label: Telegram bot and natural-language logging
surfaces.summary: Telegram command handling, OpenAI-backed parsing, report generation, scheduler integration, mailer, and realtime update paths.
driftRisks: Parser or bot commands diverge from dashboard data semantics.
Scheduled reports or notifications change without operator-facing docs.

surfaces.label: Alexa skill and account-linking surface
surfaces.summary: Alexa manifest, interaction model, skill assets, account-linking docs, reviewer-safe testing path, and related endpoint/version behavior.
driftRisks: Alexa interaction model or account-linking behavior drifts from the Express endpoint.
Store/reviewer metadata falls behind product naming, privacy, or testing reality.

surfaces.label: Release, deployment, and accountability
surfaces.summary: Package version, changelog, Railway deployment config, Nixpacks build behavior, health checks, and Mission Control release receipts.
driftRisks: Production version, changelog, and Mission Control receipts diverge.
Deployment config changes without a matching health/version verification path.

surfaces.label: Product architecture and care-tool docs
surfaces.summary: Human-readable design notes for tenancy, Clerk auth, native app readiness, account linking, and family-care product evolution.
driftRisks: Architecture docs describe planned behavior after implementation has shifted.
Care-tool naming and scope drift away from the GlideChart product structure in Mission Control.

beforeSignificantChange: Read this map and the listed docs for touched surfaces
Use the active Mission Control work packet before object-centered execution
Check whether the change touches product, data, Alexa, deployment, or docs surfaces together
afterSignificantChange: Record Mission Control receipts or proposals at transition points
